text: Data validation is the activity of deciding whether a certain data set is good for a certain purpose. By formalizing the requirements that govern this decision, it becomes possible to make the requirements unambiguous, automate the decision, and bring the decision process itself to maintainable and checkable form. This article attempts to formalize the data validation concept and some of its consequent and implications. In particular, we show how the formalization can be used to classify data quality requirements and hence to arrange them in order of increasing complexity. Some interesting effects of combining these requirements appear along the way.
